diagrams.title: Time Complexity Growth Curves
diagrams.description: Graph showing how different time complexities grow as input size increases
elements: Logarithmic O(log n) - slowly rising curve
Linearithmic O(n log n) - steeper curve
diagrams.purpose: Visualize relative efficiency and understand why lower complexity algorithms scale better

diagrams.title: Big O Notation Visualization
diagrams.description: Visual representation of asymptotic bounds using function graphs
elements: Upper bound (Big O): f(n) below c·g(n) ceiling
Lower bound (Big Omega): f(n) above c·g(n) floor
Tight bound (Big Theta): f(n) sandwiched between floors and ceilings
diagrams.purpose: Understand mathematical meaning of each notation visually

diagrams.title: Nested Loop Iteration Count
diagrams.description: Diagram showing iteration patterns for nested loops
elements: Single loop: n iterations shown as horizontal line of n boxes
Two nested loops: n×n shown as square grid of n² boxes
Three nested loops: n³ shown as cube representation
Triangular pattern for dependent nested loops (i to j)
diagrams.purpose: Visualize how nested loops contribute to quadratic and cubic complexity

diagrams.title: Best, Worst, Average Case Visualization
diagrams.description: Different input scenarios affecting algorithm performance
elements: Best case input: sorted data for search (element at start)
Average case input: random data distribution
Worst case input: reverse sorted data (element at end or not present)
diagrams.purpose: Understand why different inputs yield different complexities for same algorithm
